newsSince: Materially negative. On June 9-10 SMCI launched a ~$7.0B dilutive equity/equity-linked financing to fund components for ~$39B of AI-server orders that are explicitly non-binding and cancellable. Goldman reiterated Sell and cut its PT to $26, flagging 27% single-customer concentration (~39% YTD) and eroding margins. The March DOJ export-control indictment of three associated individuals remains an overhang. Stock -16.4% since June 9.

sources.label: DOJ export-control indictment context (Simply Wall St)

verdicts.rankRationale: On a passives/power-bottleneck lens SMCI was already peripheral, and the $7B dilutive raise plus cancellable $39B order book and Goldman's 27%-customer concentration warning directly attack the 'tight-supply, central-node' claim. Residual alpha vs passives names worsens.
verdicts.bull: $39B order pipeline signals real AI-server demand if it converts.
verdicts.neutral: Integrator margins thin; not a true passives/power bottleneck holder.
verdicts.bear: Dilution, cancellable orders, customer concentration, DOJ overhang compound.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Order book converting to firm, funded revenue

verdicts.rankRationale: This page's thesis says 'watch AI server platforms execution and pricing' and execution broke again: dilutive $7B raise, Goldman Sell to $26 on 27% concentration and margin erosion, non-binding $39B orders, DOJ overhang. New negative evidence justifies stepping down on the under-owned-bottleneck master list.
verdicts.bull: Cheaper post-selloff; scaled deployment exposure if demand holds.
verdicts.neutral: Commoditized integrator; pricing power and margins remain the swing factor.
verdicts.bear: Dilution plus concentration plus governance/export overhang erodes the bottleneck case.
